title: Schedule Pod with nodeSelector
description:
Label a node with `disktype=ssd` and deploy a Pod that only schedules on nodes carrying that label. Then observe what happens when the label is removed — the next pod will be stuck Pending because no node matches the selector.

**Common Pitfalls:** `nodeSelector` is a hard constraint. If no node has the required label, the pod stays Pending forever. Use `kubectl describe pod` to see the scheduling failure reason in Events.
steps: Label a node: kubectl label node <node-name> disktype=ssd
Define a Pod 'ssd-app' with nodeSelector: disktype: ssd
Apply the manifest and verify the pod is Running on the labeled node
Check placement: kubectl get pod ssd-app -o wide
Remove the label: kubectl label node <node-name> disktype-
Delete and recreate the pod — observe it stays Pending due to unmatched nodeSelector
hints: kubectl get nodes shows available node names
nodeSelector is a map directly under spec — same level as containers
kubectl get pod ssd-app -o wide shows the Node column
Use kubectl describe pod ssd-app to read scheduling failure events
Set disktype to 'ssd' in nodeSelector to match the label you added to the node

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ssd-app
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
  nodeSelector:
    disktype: # TODO: use 'ssd'

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ssd-app
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
  nodeSelector:
    disktype: ssd
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo 'Checking Pod ssd-app exists...'
if ! kubectl get pod ssd-app &>/dev/null; then
  echo '✗ Pod ssd-app not found'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ Pod ssd-app exists'

echo 'Checking Pod has nodeSelector disktype=ssd...'
SELECTOR=$(kubectl get pod ssd-app -o jsonpath='{.spec.nodeSelector.disktype}')
if [ "$SELECTOR" != "ssd" ]; then
  echo "✗ nodeSelector disktype expected 'ssd', got '$SELECTOR'"
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ nodeSelector disktype=ssd is set'

echo 'Checking if any node has disktype=ssd label...'
SSD_NODES=$(kubectl get nodes -l disktype=ssd --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
if [ "$SSD_NODES" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "✓ Found $SSD_NODES node(s) with disktype=ssd"
  echo 'Waiting for Pod ssd-app to be Running...'
  kubectl wait pod ssd-app --for=condition=Ready --timeout=60s
  echo '✓ Pod ssd-app is Running'
  ASSIGNED_NODE=$(kubectl get pod ssd-app -o jsonpath='{.spec.nodeName}')
  NODE_LABEL=$(kubectl get node "$ASSIGNED_NODE" -o jsonpath='{.metadata.labels.disktype}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$NODE_LABEL" = "ssd" ]; then
    echo "✓ Pod is running on node with disktype=ssd: $ASSIGNED_NODE"
  else
    echo '✗ Pod is NOT running on a disktype=ssd node'
    exit 1
  fi
else
  echo 'No node with disktype=ssd found — pod will be Pending (expected if testing label removal)'
  POD_PHASE=$(kubectl get pod ssd-app -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}')
  if [ "$POD_PHASE" = "Pending" ]; then
    echo '✓ Pod is correctly Pending when no matching node exists'
  fi
fi

echo ''
echo 'All checks passed!'
exit 0
